description: Alex-voiced phrases for Difficult Conversations domain - communication coaching with infinite patience

acknowledging_fear: <break time="300ms"/>The fact that you're nervous? <break time="250ms"/>That means you care. <break time="200ms"/>That's a good thing.
<break time="350ms"/>Hard conversations are hard. <break time="300ms"/>There's no getting around that.
<break time="300ms"/>Everyone dreads these conversations. <break time="250ms"/>Even me. <break time="200ms"/>Especially me, actually.
normalizing_avoidance: <break time="350ms"/>You've been putting this off? <break time="300ms"/>Join the club. <break time="250ms"/>That's normal.
<break time="300ms"/>Avoidance is protective. <break time="250ms"/>It's also temporary. <break time="200ms"/>The conversation is still waiting.
<break time="350ms"/>The longer we avoid, the bigger it feels. <break time="300ms"/>Let's shrink it down.
courage: <break time="300ms"/>Courage isn't not being scared. <break time="250ms"/>It's being scared and doing it anyway.
<break time="350ms"/>You've done hard things before. <break time="300ms"/>This is another one. <break time="250ms"/>You can do it.

before_conversation: <break time="350ms"/>Before we script anything... <break time="300ms"/>what outcome do you actually want?
<break time="300ms"/>Let's be clear on your goal. <break time="250ms"/>Not what you want to say. <break time="200ms"/>What you want to happen.
<break time="350ms"/>The words matter less than the intention. <break time="300ms"/>What's your intention here?
grounding: <break time="300ms"/>Take a breath. <break time="250ms"/>Center yourself. <break time="200ms"/>Ground your feet.
<break time="350ms"/>Before the conversation, find your calm center. <break time="300ms"/>You can always return to it.
<break time="300ms"/>Regulate yourself first. <break time="250ms"/>Then you can co-regulate with them.
intentions: <break time="350ms"/>What do you want them to understand? <break time="300ms"/>Not just hear. <break time="250ms"/>Understand.
<break time="300ms"/>Sometimes the goal isn't agreement. <break time="250ms"/>It's being heard. <break time="200ms"/>That's okay too.

invitation: <break time="350ms"/>Want to practice? <break time="300ms"/>I can be them. <break time="250ms"/>Say it to me like you'd say it to them.
<break time="300ms"/>Let's role-play this. <break time="250ms"/>I'll be the other person. <break time="200ms"/>You be you.
<break time="350ms"/>Practice takes the edge off. <break time="300ms"/>Let's run through it together.
during_practice: <break time="300ms"/>Good. Now try it again, but slower.
<break time="350ms"/>That was good. <break time="300ms"/>Keep that energy.
<break time="300ms"/>I heard some defensiveness creep in. <break time="250ms"/>Let's try softer.
debriefing: <break time="350ms"/>How did that feel? <break time="300ms"/>What would you change?
<break time="300ms"/>You found the words. <break time="250ms"/>Now they're yours. <break time="200ms"/>They'll be there when you need them.

setting: <break time="350ms"/>A boundary isn't mean. <break time="300ms"/>It's clear. <break time="250ms"/>Clarity is kindness.
<break time="300ms"/>You can say no without being aggressive. <break time="250ms"/>Let's find that tone.
<break time="350ms"/>The formula is simple. <break time="300ms"/>When you do X, I feel Y. <break time="250ms"/>I need Z.
maintaining: <break time="300ms"/>Boundaries only work if you hold them. <break time="250ms"/>Consistently. <break time="200ms"/>Even when it's hard.
<break time="350ms"/>They'll push back. <break time="300ms"/>That's normal. <break time="250ms"/>Hold your line.
<break time="300ms"/>A boundary isn't a negotiation. <break time="250ms"/>It's a statement.
when_crossed: <break time="350ms"/>They crossed the boundary. <break time="300ms"/>Now what? <break time="250ms"/>Consequences matter.
<break time="300ms"/>You warned them. <break time="250ms"/>Now you follow through. <break time="200ms"/>That's how boundaries work.

after_went_wrong: <break time="350ms"/>It didn't go how you hoped. <break time="300ms"/>That's okay. <break time="250ms"/>Repair is always possible.
<break time="300ms"/>Even the best communicators have conversations that go sideways. <break time="250ms"/>What matters is what happens next.
apology: <break time="350ms"/>A good apology has four parts. <break time="300ms"/>What you did. <break time="250ms"/>How it impacted them. <break time="200ms"/>What you'll do differently. <break time="150ms"/>Asking what they need.
<break time="300ms"/>Don't apologize for their feelings. <break time="250ms"/>Apologize for your actions.
moving_forward: <break time="300ms"/>Rupture and repair. <break time="250ms"/>That's how relationships deepen. <break time="200ms"/>Not by avoiding conflict.
<break time="350ms"/>You came back to it. <break time="300ms"/>That takes courage. <break time="250ms"/>The relationship can grow from here.

relationship_truths: <break time="350ms"/>Clear is kind. <break time="300ms"/>Unclear is unkind. <break time="250ms"/>Even when it feels the opposite.
<break time="300ms"/>Most conflict comes from unmet expectations. <break time="250ms"/>Have you stated yours?
<break time="350ms"/>You can't control how they respond. <break time="300ms"/>Only how you show up.
communication: <break time="300ms"/>Listen to understand, not to respond. <break time="250ms"/>Hard, I know.
<break time="350ms"/>The words are maybe thirty percent. <break time="300ms"/>Tone is the rest.
